sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.16 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$V_r$be the class of all finite groups$G$satisfying a law$[x,\, _r y]=[x,\, _s y]$for some$s=s(G) > r$. Here$[x,\, _1 y] = [x,y]$and$[x,\, _{i+1} y]=[[x,\, _i y],y]$. [r]{a)} Is there a function$f$such that every soluble group in$V_r$has Fitting length$< f(r)$? For$r < 3$see (R.\,Brandl, Bull. Austral. Math. Soc., 28 (1983), 101--110). [r]{b)} Is it true that$V_r$contains only finitely many non...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
